description: This map displays a False Color Infrared (NIR) view of a landscape, likely illustrating land cover classifications. Land parcels are delineated by yellow lines and identified by four-digit codes. The large central parcel, labelled "2330", corresponds to "Sclerophyllous vegetation" in the CORINE Land Cover (CLC) nomenclature. Visually, this area shows distinct linear patterns, indicative of agricultural fields with varying vegetation vigour, represented by bright red to lighter green/cyan tones. Two other parcels, labelled "4100", which correspond to "Inland marshes" in the CLC nomenclature, are visible and appear uniformly bright red, suggesting dense, healthy vegetation. White lines delineate a dark blue water body (likely a river or canal) along the right and bottom right edges of the image, as well as thinner linear features, possibly roads or smaller canals. In this false-colour representation, bright red indicates vigorous vegetation, while lighter green/cyan indicates less vigorous vegetation or different land cover types, and dark blue indicates water. No scale, compass, legend, or date is visible.
